pattern: Tracking each live token by its absolute expiry time turns generate/renew into hash-map writes and reduces counting to filtering out entries whose expiry has already passed.
hints: A token created or renewed at time t is alive only while currentTime is strictly before t + timeToLive; store that expiry instead of the creation time.
renew must do nothing if the token is missing or already expired at the current time — check both before touching it.
Keep one map from token id to expiry time; generate and renew overwrite the expiry, and counting walks the map for entries whose expiry exceeds the queried time.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: renew on missing token is ignored; expired token cannot be renewed
a token queried exactly at its expiry time counts as expired
renew right before expiry extends the lifetime
renew exactly at expiry instant is too late and must be ignored
count with no tokens at all
several tokens with staggered expiries; count reflects only those still alive
renew a still-live token then count after the original window would have closed

stress.description: long sequence of generate/renew/count with strictly increasing times and unique generated ids
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ttl = rng.randint(5, 50)
    ops = ['AuthenticationManager']
    args = [[ttl]]
    t = 1
    counter = 0
    live = []
    for _ in range(size):
        t += rng.randint(1, 10)
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.5:
            counter += 1
            tid = 't' + str(counter)
            if len(tid) > 5:
                tid = tid[:5]
            # ensure uniqueness within length cap by padding via base mapping
            tid = format(counter, 'x')
            if len(tid) > 5:
                # restart counter space rarely; keep generate ids unique by skipping
                continue
            ops.append('generate')
            args.append([tid, t])
            live.append(tid)
        elif r < 0.75 and live:
            tid = rng.choice(live)
            ops.append('renew')
            args.append([tid, t])
        else:
            ops.append('countUnexpiredTokens')
            args.append([t])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: generate ids are hex of a counter so they stay unique and within 5 chars; times strictly increase
